title: A robust and tunable Luttinger liquid in correlated edge of transition-metal second-order topological insulator Ta2Pd3Te5
classification_notes: GPT error: Expecting value: line 172 column 7 (char 11750)

paper_analysis.summary: Ta2Pd3Te5 is a layered transition-metal compound identified as a 2D second-order topological insulator whose edge states host a robust, tunable Luttinger liquid. Using DFT/ARPES plus extensive low-temperature electrical transport on thin flakes and bulk devices, the paper shows coexisting bulk insulating behavior, in-gap edge conduction, and an edge gap, with a gate- and thickness-dependent phase diagram spanning LL to Fermi-liquid-like regimes.
measurement_types: differential conductance dI/dV vs bias and temperature

figures.description: Crystal structure of Ta2Pd3Te5 viewed along the chain direction, with an inset optical image of a needle-like single crystal.
Cross-sectional STEM images along [010] and [001] showing layered/quasi-1D structure and monolayer thickness ~0.7 nm.
ARPES intensity plot along Γ–Y showing the band structure and conduction-band bottom above EF after Fermi-Dirac division.
Photon-energy-dependent ARPES intensity at EF-0.05 eV showing weak interlayer coupling.
Schematic of double-band inversion along the Y–Γ line.
Modified band structure with added onsite energy to simulate interaction-induced gap opening.
Calculated 1D gapped edge-state spectrum of the 2D quadrupole topological insulator.
Schematic showing gate-tuned Fermi level shift with back-gate voltage.
Device #A1 differential conductance dI/dV versus back-gate voltage at 0.42 K, with optical image and measurement schematic inset.
Device #A2 differential conductance dI/dV versus back-gate voltage at 6.01 K, with optical image and measurement schematic inset.
Log-log dI/dV versus DC bias for device #A1 at CNP and multiple temperatures, showing bulk gap, power-law LL region, and low-bias edge gap.
Log-log dI/dV versus DC bias for device #A2 at 6.01 K and multiple back-gate voltages, showing evolution from three-region to two-region behavior.
Temperature dependence of dI/dV for device #A2 at different gate voltages, with Arrhenius fits in the inset.
Hall-bar nonlocal measurement geometry on a thin film, with optical image inset.
Local differential conductance dI0/dV0 versus back-gate voltage at different temperatures for device #A3.
Nonlocal voltage ratio Vnl1/V0 versus back-gate voltage at different temperatures.
Nonlocal voltage ratio Vnl2/V0 versus back-gate voltage at different temperatures.
Device #A4 dI/dV versus DC bias at Vbg = 0 V for multiple temperatures, showing LL power-law behavior and low-T edge gap.
Scaled conductance collapse [(dI/dV)/T^0.69 vs eVDC/kBT] demonstrating universal LL scaling.
Gate-tunable LL exponent alpha extracted from transport data across devices.
Temperature-dependent dI/dV and gate sweep for device #B1 showing LL without a distinct edge gap.
Device #B1 dI/dV versus DC bias at multiple temperatures.
Scaled conductance collapse for device #B1 demonstrating LL scaling.
Bulk sample #C1 dI/dV versus temperature.
Bulk sample #C1 dI/dV versus DC bias at multiple temperatures.
Scaled conductance collapse for bulk sample #C1.
Temperature-dependent bulk conductivity for type B and type C samples.
Temperature-dependent edge conductivity for type B and type C samples.
Starting resistance of LL behavior versus edge square number, showing approximate linear scaling over five orders of magnitude.
Thickness-dependent bulk gap extracted from dI/dV(T) Arrhenius analysis.
Thickness-dependent LL phase diagram with transition temperatures T1/T1*/T1** and T2.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include ARPES intensity maps, STEM images, and extensive electrical transport traces from thin-film and bulk devices: dI/dV versus DC bias, dI/dV versus temperature, dI/dV versus back-gate voltage, nonlocal voltages, and resistance/conductivity versus temperature. The paper also mentions optical images of devices and Hall-bar/nonlocal configurations. The raw data were generated using cryostats, lock-in measurements, and gate/bias sources.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that all data needed to evaluate the conclusions are present in the main text and/or Supplementary Information, and that all raw data generated in the study have been deposited in Figshare at https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.24407743.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described as the measurements generated in this study and deposited in Figshare. The paper does not specify file formats, but the underlying raw modalities are ARPES spectra/maps, STEM images, and transport measurement traces from cryostats using Keithley sources and lock-in detection.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include extracted gaps from Arrhenius fits, power-law exponents from log-log conductance fits, scaled-collapse plots, conductivity/edge-conductivity curves, and phase-diagram transition temperatures versus thickness. The paper explicitly describes fitting procedures and derived quantities such as bulk gap Δ, edge gap Ee, and LL exponent α.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-ready data are clearly provided in the main figures and Supplementary Information, and the authors state that raw data are deposited. The text also notes that raw data are included in Supplementary Fig. 12 for some exponent extraction and that supplementary figures contain additional systematic datasets.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is multimodal and mixes experimental raw measurements with derived/fit parameters and theoretical band-structure calculations. The paper distinguishes between raw transport traces and processed quantities such as exponents, gaps, and phase boundaries. Supplementary figures are important for the full dataset, especially for repeated devices and raw data supporting exponent extraction. The repository is explicitly described as raw data deposited on Figshare, but no separate statement about source-data files or scripts is given.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The authors state that 'all of the raw data generated in this study have been deposited in Figshare.'
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper presents many figure-specific extracted quantities and fit results (gaps, exponents, scaled collapses, phase diagrams) and says all data needed to evaluate the conclusions are present in the main text and/or Supplementary Information.
data_availability_section_relevant: All data needed to evaluate the conclusions in the paper are present in the main text and/or the Supplementary Information.
all of the raw data generated in this study have been deposited in Figshare
Raw data are included in Supplementary Fig. 12
The error bars reflect the uncertainty due to measurement noise
The gray dot dash lines show the power-law behavior
The yellow dot dash lines are fitting curves of Arrhenius equation
priority_modalities: transport traces (dI/dV vs VDC, T, Vbg)
derived fit parameters (gap, exponent, phase boundaries)
